Name: Dialga
Effects: Draw a card.
You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

Name: Dusknoir
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if your opponent has 4 or more Benched Pokémon, you may choose 1 of them and shuffle that Pokémon and all cards attached to it into his or her deck. This power can’t be used if Dusknoir is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Put 5 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. Then, count the number of Prize cards your opponent has taken and put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

Name: Electivire
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), if Elekid is anywhere under Electivire, you may move a [L] Energy attached to your Pokémon to Electivire. This power can’t be used if Electivire is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard all [L] Energy attached to Electivire. If you do, this attack’s base damage is 120 instead of 60.
Flavor-Text: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

Name: Infernape
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Discard all [R] Energy attached to Infernape.
Flavor-Text: It uses a special kind of martial arts involving all its limbs. Its fire never goes out.

Name: Lucario
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to sense the auras of all things. It understands human speech.

Name: Luxray
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Luxray from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at your opponent’s hand. If your opponent’s Bench isn’t full, choose 1 Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s hand, and put it onto his or her Bench. Then, switch it with the Defending Pokémon.
Effects: Move all [L] Energy attached to Luxray to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Ignore this effect if you don’t have any Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

Name: Magnezone
Pok\u00e9-BODY-Effect: If you have any [M] Energy attached to your Active Pokémon, the Retreat Cost for that Pokémon is 0.
Effects: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to Magnezone.
Flavor-Text: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

Name: Manaphy
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Switch Manaphy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Born on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Count the number of your Pokémon that have any damage counters on them. Put that many damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.
Does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations.

Name: Palkia
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Energy attached to Palkia and put the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent’s deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

Name: Rhyperior
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Rhyperior from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard the top 3 cards from your opponent’s deck.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance. Rhyperior can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It puts rocks in holes in its palms and uses its muscles to shoot them. Geodude are shot at rare times.

Name: Roserade
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If Budew is anywhere under Roserade, choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

Name: Shiftry
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Shiftry is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Evolved Pokémon on your opponent’s Bench, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from that Pokémon, and put it back into his or her hand. This power can’t be used if Shiftry is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

Name: Skuntank
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that doesn’t have any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all damage done to Staraptor by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s) until the end of your next turn.
Flip a coin. If tails, Staraptor does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.

Name: Torterra
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Remove 2 damage counters from each of your [G] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Azumarill during your opponent’s next turn.

If Azumarill has 3 or more Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage. If Azurill is anywhere under Azumarill, flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lives in rivers and lakes. In water, its coloring and patterns trick the vision of foes.

Name: Beautifly
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
After your attack, remove from Beautifly the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has an aggressive nature. It stabs prey with its long, narrow mouth to drain the prey’s fluids.

Name: Bibarel
Effects: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Bibarel. Bibarel is now Asleep.
Bibarel does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

Name: Carnivine
Effects: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Carnivine. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Carnivine’s, this attack does 60 damage instead.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. Metronome copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else in order to use that attack.) Clefable performs that attack.
Flavor-Text: Rarely seen by people, it is said to be drawn by the full moon to play at deserted lakes.

Name: Drapion
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Search your discard pile for up to 5 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards. Show them to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.
Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It’s drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

Name: Dustox
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Floatzel but not used to pay for this attack’s Energy cost. You can’t add more than 40 damage in this way.
Flavor-Text: It floats using its well-developed floatation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

Name: Gengar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put damage counters on the Defending Pokémon until it is 10 HP away from being Knocked Out.
Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. Then, switch Gengar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It hides in shadows. It is said that if Gengar is hiding, it cools that area by nearly 10 degrees F.

Name: Heracross
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It gathers in forests to search for tree sap, its favorite food. It’s strong enough to hurl foes.

Name: Hippowdon
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

Name: Lopunny
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. Remove a number of damage counters equal to the number of heads from 1 of your Pokémon.
Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) You may switch Lopunny with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An extremely cautious Pokémon. It cloaks its body with its fluffy ear fur when it senses danger.

Name: Machamp
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 20 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It punches with its four arms at blinding speed. It can launch 1,000 punches in two seconds.

Name: Medicham
Effects: Count the number of damage counters on Medicham. Put that many damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Medicham does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Through yoga training, it has honed its sixth sense. Its movements are elegant.

Name: Munchlax
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Snorlax from your hand onto Munchlax (this counts as evolving Munchlax) and remove all damage counters from Munchlax.
Effects: Discard 2 cards from your hand. (If you can’t discard 2 cards, this attack does nothing.) Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It wolfs down its weight in food once a day, swallowing food whole with almost no chewing.

Name: Noctowl
Effects: Choose a card from your hand and put it as a Prize card face up. Then, choose 1 of your face-down Prize cards without looking and put it into your hand. This attack does nothing if all of your Prize cards are face up.
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its eyes are special. They can pick out objects as long as there is the tiniest amount of light.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If tails, Pachirisu does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

Name: Purugly
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking and discard it.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon’s nest and claims it as its own.

Name: Snorlax
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Snorlax is now Asleep. If Snorlax is evolved from Munchlax, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach can digest any kind of food, even if it happens to be moldy or rotten.

Name: Steelix
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Steelix can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Tempered underground under high pressure and heat, its body is harder than any metal.

Name: Vespiquen
Effects: Discard a [G] Energy attached to Vespiquen and remove all damage counters from 1 of your Benched [G] Pokémon.
Does 10 damage times the number of [G] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s).
Flavor-Text: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by COMBEE.

Name: Weavile
Effects: Search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Does 10 damage times the total amount of [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

Name: Wobbuffet
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, move all damage counters from Wobbuffet to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It desperately tries to keep its black tail hidden. It is said to be proof the tail hides a secret.

Name: Wynaut
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Wobbuffet from your hand onto Wynaut (this counts as evolving Wynaut) and remove all damage counters from Wynaut.
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flavor-Text: It grows strong by pushing up against others en masse. It loves eating sweet fruit.

Name: Budew
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Roselia from your hand onto Budew (this counts as evolving Budew) and remove all damage counters from Budew.
Effects: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.

Name: Cascoon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if Cascoon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to Cascoon if that damage is 30 or less.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is hot inside its cocoon. All the cells in its body create the energy for it to evolve.

Name: Cherrim
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage and remove 3 damage counters from Cherrim.
Flavor-Text: It blooms during times of strong sunlight. It tries to make up for everything it endured as a bud.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 of your Benched Pokémon and all cards attached to on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon. If tails, put 2 damage counters on 1 of your Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that doesn’t have a Retreat Cost. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its body is hollow. It is said that those who look into its body are sucked into the void.

Name: Elekid
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Electabuzz from your hand onto Elekid (this counts as evolving Elekid) and remove all damage counters from Elekid.
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It generates electricity by whirling its arms. However, it can’t store the energy it makes.

Name: Grotle
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

Name: Haunter
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It can slip through any obstacle. It lurks inside walls to keep an eye on its foes.

Name: Hippopotas
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid places. Instead of perspiration, it expels grainy sand from its body.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

Name: Machoke
Effects: Does 40 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.
Flavor-Text: MACHOKE’s boundless power is very dangerous, so it wears a belt that suppresses its energy.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it and switch that Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is actually three MAGNEMITE linked by magnetism. A group can set off a magnetic storm.

Name: Mantyke
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Mantine from your hand onto Mantyke (this counts as evolving Mantyke) and remove all damage counters from Mantyke.
Effects: Search your deck for a [W] Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: A friendly Pokémon that captures the subtle flows of seawater using its two antennae.

Name: Monferno
Effects: Search your discard pile for a [R] Energy card and attach it to Monferno.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: To intimidate attackers, it stretches the fire on its tail to make itself appear bigger.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

Name: Prinplup
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Rapidash and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

Name: Rhydon
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.
Rhydon does 10 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.
Flavor-Text: Its brain developed after it stood up on its hind legs. Its drill horn bores tunnels through solid rock.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

Name: Seaking
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Seaking.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by hollowing out boulders in streams with its horn. It defends its eggs with its life.

Name: Silcoon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, If Silcoon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to Silcoon if that damage is 30 or less.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It anchors itself by wrapping twigs with the silk from its body. It motionlessly awaits evolution.

Name: Staravia
Effects: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

Name: Unown A
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown A is on your Bench, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown B
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown B is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, discard all cards attached to any 1 of your Unown and shuffle that Pokémon back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown C
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown C is on your Bench, you may flip coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 Unown and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Unown D
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown D is on your Bench, you may flip a coin. If heads, each player may draw a card. (You draw your card first.)
Flavor-Text: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

Name: Azurill
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Marill from your hand onto Azurill (this counts a evolving Azurill) and remove all damage counters from Azurill.
Effects: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon that lives by water. It moves quickly on land by bouncing on its big tail.

Name: Bidoof
Effects: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Search your discard pile for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.
Flavor-Text: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bonsly
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Sudowoodo from your hand onto Bonsly (this counts as evolving Bonsly) and remove all damage counters from Bonsly.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can’t play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent’s next turn, and any damage done to Bonsly by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body’s fluid levels by eliminating excess.

Name: Buizel
Effects: If Buizel has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

Name: Buneary
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

Name: Chatot
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Cherubi
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If this attack does any damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), remove 1 damage counter from Cherubi.
Flavor-Text: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to Chimchar.
Flavor-Text: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

Name: Clefairy
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
If Clefairy is evolved from Cleffa, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Thought to live with others on quiet mountains, it is popular for its adorable nature.

Name: Cleffa
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Clefairy from your hand onto Cleffa (this counts as evolving Cleffa) and remove all damage counters from Cleffa.
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Its silhouette is like a star. It is believed to arrive riding on shooting stars.

Name: Combee
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon formed by three others. It busily carries sweet floral honey to VESPIQUEN.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks. That Pokémon can’t use that attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, Electabuzz does 10 damage to itself.

Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If Electabuzz is evolved from Elekid, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Half of all blackouts occur when this Pokémon appears at power plants and eats electricity.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Move 1 damage counter from Gastly to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s body is 95% made up of gases, which are blown away by strong gusts of wind.

Name: Glameow
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness if very popular among some.

Name: Goldeen
Effects: Goldeen does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It swims elegantly by flittering its tail as if it were a dress. It has the look of a queen.

Name: Hoothoot
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It always stands on one foot. Even when attacked, it does not brace itself using both feet.

Name: Machop
Flavor-Text: It hefts a GRAVELER repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

Name: Magnemite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
If the Defending Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The units at its sides generate electromagnetic waves that keep it airborne. It feeds on electricity.

Name: Marill
Effects: If Marill is evolved from Azurill, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Switch Marill with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its tail as a float, it dives underwater. It likes eating plants that grow on river bottoms.

Name: Meditite
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Meditite during your opponent’s next turn.
Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It eats just one berry a day. By enduring hunger, its spirit is tempered and made sharper.

Name: Mime Jr.
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Mr. Mime from your hand onto Mime Jr. (this counts as evolving Mime Jr.) and remove all damage counters from Mime Jr.
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It habitually mimics foes. Once mimicked, the foe cannot take its eyes off this Pokémon.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Choose 1 card from your opponent’s hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It loves to sneak up on people late at night, then startle them with its shrieklike cry.

Name: Onix
Effects: Choose up to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Onix can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When it travels underground, it causes rumbling and tremors. It can move at 50 mph.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Ponyta
Flavor-Text: About an hour after birth, its fiery mane and tail grow out, giving it an impressive appearance.

Name: Rhyhorn
Effects: Rhyhorn does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its body is clad in a thick hide, and its tackles topple buildings. Unfortunately, it is not smart.

Name: Roselia
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If Roselia is evolved from Budew, this attack does 10 damage to the Defending Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: ROSELIA raised on clean drinking water are known to grow vividly colored flowers.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, if Seedot would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, Seedot is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Skorupi
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It feeds on eggs stolen from nests. Its sharply hooked claws rip vulnerable spots on prey.

Name: Starly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

Name: Stunky
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

Name: Wurmple
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It loves to eat leaves. If it is attacked by a STARLY, it will defend itself with its spiked rear.

Name: Double Full Heal
Description: Remove all Special Conditions from each of your Active Pokémon.

Name: Energy Restore
Description: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand. If you don’t have that many basic Energy cards in your discard pile, put all of them into your hand.

Name: Energy Switch
Description: Move a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

Name: Night Pokémon Center
Description: Choose 1 of your Pokémon. Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, remove all damage counters from that Pokémon. If both are tails, discard all Energy cards attached to that Pokémon.

Name: PlusPower
Description: Attach PlusPower to 1 of your Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of your turn.If the Pokémon PlusPower is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Poké Ball
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Pokédex HANDY910is
Description: Look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card on the bottom of your deck.

Name: Professor Rowan
Description: Choose 1 card in your hand and shuffle the rest of your cards into your deck. Then, draw 4 cards. (If this is the only card in your hand, you can’t play this card.)
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Rival
Description: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. Your opponent chooses 3 of those cards. Put those cards into your hand and put other 2 cards on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Supporter-Rule: You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Name: Speed Stadium
Description: Once during each player’s turn, the player may flip a coin until he or she gets tails. For each heads, that player draws a card.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Super Scoop Up
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, return 1 of your Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

Name: Warp Point
Description: Your opponent switches 1 of his or her Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any. You switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Name: Potion
Description: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if that Pokémon has only 1).

Name: Switch
Description: Switch 1 of your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Empoleon LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose up to 2 cards from your opponent’s hand without looking and put them face down next to the Defending Pokémon. (These cards are not in play or in your opponent’s hand.) At the end of your opponent’s next turn, return those cards to your opponent’s hand. This power can’t be used if Empoleon is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage to that Pokémon (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Empoleon can’t attack during your next turn.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Empoleon. Empoleon LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Infernape LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Discard the other 2 cards. This power can’t be used if Infernape is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Search your discard pile for 8 [R] Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and shuffle them into your deck. (This attack does nothing if you don’t have 8 [R] Energy cards in your discard pile.)
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Infernape. Infernape LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

Name: Torterra LV.X
Pok\u00e9-POWER-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have more Prize cards left than your opponent, you may choose 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This power can’t be used if Torterra is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Torterra does 30 damage to itself.
Level-Up-Rule: Put this card onto your Active Torterra. Torterra LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
